name: vitest
context: Write and run tests with Vitest, the Vite-native test framework — blazing fast, native ESM/TypeScript, Jest-compatible API, coverage, UI, and browser mode
info: Vitest is the Vite-native unit test framework designed for speed and modern JavaScript/TypeScript projects. It leverages Vite's transform pipeline for native ESM, TypeScript, and JSX support without configuration. Vitest provides a Jest-compatible API (describe, it, expect, vi.fn, vi.mock) so migration from Jest is straightforward. Key capabilities include watch mode with HMR-based re-runs, in-source testing, workspace support for monorepos, browser mode via @vitest/browser, V8 and Istanbul coverage providers, a browser-based UI dashboard via @vitest/ui, snapshot and inline snapshot testing, and compile-time type assertions with expectTypeOf. Vitest 3.x (released December 2024) is the latest major version. Configuration lives in vitest.config.ts or inside the test block of vite.config.ts. See [[testing/jest]] for the Jest equivalent, [[testing/mocking]] for cross-framework mocking strategies, [[testing/patterns]] for structural test patterns, and [[testing/ci]] for running Vitest in CI pipelines.

sections.title: Quick start — install and first test
sections.body: Install Vitest as a dev dependency: `npm install -D vitest`. Create a test file next to your source code (e.g., `src/math.test.ts`). Import `describe`, `it`, `expect` from `vitest`. Run `npx vitest` for watch mode or `npx vitest run` for a single pass. Vitest discovers files matching `**/*.{test,spec}.{ts,tsx,js,jsx}` by default. Add `"test": "vitest"` to package.json scripts. For existing Vite projects, Vitest reuses your vite.config.ts — just add a `test` block. No babel, no ts-jest, no transform configuration needed.

sections.title: Configuration — vitest.config.ts options
sections.body: Vitest config lives in `vitest.config.ts` (standalone) or the `test` block of `vite.config.ts`. Key options: `include`/`exclude` for test file patterns, `environment` for DOM simulation (node, jsdom, happy-dom), `globals` to expose test APIs without imports, `setupFiles` for bootstrap code, `coverage` for provider and thresholds, `pool` for execution strategy (threads, forks, vmThreads), `testTimeout`/`hookTimeout` for limits, `reporters` for output format, `retry` for flaky test retries. Use `defineConfig` from `vitest/config` for type safety. Per-file environment overrides are supported via `// @vitest-environment jsdom` comments.

sections.title: Mocking — vi.fn, vi.mock, vi.spyOn
sections.body: Vitest provides the `vi` object for all mocking needs. `vi.fn()` creates a mock function that tracks calls and can return configured values via `mockReturnValue`, `mockResolvedValue`, or `mockImplementation`. `vi.mock('module')` mocks an entire module — it is auto-hoisted to the top of the file. `vi.spyOn(obj, 'method')` wraps an existing method to track calls while preserving the original implementation (call `mockRestore()` to undo). `vi.stubGlobal('name', value)` replaces global variables like `fetch`. Use `vi.useFakeTimers()` / `vi.advanceTimersByTime(ms)` for timer control. Clean up with `vi.clearAllMocks()`, `vi.resetAllMocks()`, or `vi.restoreAllMocks()` in `afterEach`.

sections.title: Coverage — V8 and Istanbul providers
sections.body: Install `@vitest/coverage-v8` (fast, V8 engine native) or `@vitest/coverage-istanbul` (traditional instrumentation, more accurate branch coverage). Run `npx vitest run --coverage`. Configure in vitest.config.ts under `test.coverage`: set `provider`, `reporter` (text, html, lcov, json), `include`/`exclude` patterns, and `thresholds` for lines, branches, functions, and statements. Coverage thresholds cause the process to exit non-zero when not met — ideal for CI gates. Use `lcov` reporter for integration with Codecov, Coveralls, or SonarQube.

sections.title: Workspaces — monorepo testing
sections.body: Create `vitest.workspace.ts` at the repository root to define multiple test projects. Each project can have its own environment, include patterns, and config. Reference per-package configs with a glob (`'packages/*/vitest.config.ts'`) or define inline project objects with a `name` and `test` block. Run all projects with `npx vitest`, or target one with `--project name`. Workspaces share a single Vitest process for fast startup. Use `defineWorkspace` from `vitest/config` for type safety.

sections.title: Browser mode — testing in real browsers
sections.body: Vitest browser mode runs tests in a real browser instead of jsdom or happy-dom. Install `@vitest/browser` and a browser provider (`playwright` or `webdriverio`). Configure `test.browser.enabled: true` and specify `provider: 'playwright'` with browser instances (chromium, firefox, webkit). Browser mode gives you real DOM APIs, real CSS, and real event handling — use it for component tests that need high fidelity. For basic DOM testing, jsdom or happy-dom is faster.

sections.title: Migration from Jest — switching to Vitest
sections.body: Vitest is API-compatible with Jest, so migration is mostly mechanical. Replace `jest.fn()` with `vi.fn()`, `jest.mock()` with `vi.mock()`, `jest.spyOn()` with `vi.spyOn()`. Remove ts-jest, babel-jest, and jest config — Vitest handles TypeScript and ESM natively. If using `globals: true`, the only code change is swapping `jest.*` calls to `vi.*`. Replace `jest.config.js` with `vitest.config.ts`. Update package.json scripts from `jest` to `vitest run`. Snapshot files are compatible. The `@jest/globals` import becomes `import { ... } from 'vitest'`.

sections.title: Type testing — expectTypeOf assertions
sections.body: Vitest supports compile-time type assertions via `expectTypeOf()`. Import it from `vitest` and chain assertions: `.toBeString()`, `.toBeNumber()`, `.toBeFunction()`, `.toMatchTypeOf<T>()`, `.parameter(0).toBeString()`, `.returns.toBeNumber()`. Enable with `test.typecheck.enabled: true` in config and run with `npx vitest typecheck`. Type tests run the TypeScript compiler on your test files and verify type relationships without executing runtime code. Useful for validating library public APIs, generic type utilities, and type guard correctness.

links.context: Jest test framework — Vitest is the modern alternative with compatible API
Cross-framework mocking strategies — vi.fn, vi.mock, vi.spyOn
Structural testing patterns: Arrange-Act-Assert, data-driven tests
Running Vitest in CI/CD pipelines with coverage gates
E2E browser testing — complements Vitest browser mode for full user flows
TypeScript compiler — Vitest type testing uses tsc under the hood

related.note: Jest is the legacy alternative — Vitest provides the same API with native ESM and Vite integration
Vitest evolves from and is inspired by Jest, offering a modern Vite-native replacement
Playwright handles full E2E browser testing; Vitest browser mode handles component-level browser tests
Vitest vi.* mocking maps to cross-framework mocking concepts
Testing patterns (AAA, factories, parametrize) apply directly to Vitest tests
Vitest typecheck mode uses the TypeScript compiler for type assertions

scripts.name: Run Vitest tests
scripts.description: Detect local vitest installation and run tests with support for patterns, coverage, watch, UI, and CI mode

env.TEST_PATTERN: Optional test file or name pattern to filter
env.VITEST_WATCH: Set to 'true' to enable watch mode
env.VITEST_UI: Set to 'true' to enable browser UI
env.VITEST_RUN: Set to 'true' for single-pass run mode

pattern.description: Test file or name pattern to filter (e.g., 'src/utils' or 'test_login')

watch.description: Enable watch mode for continuous re-runs

run.description: Run once and exit (CI mode, disables watch)

numSkippedTests.description: Number of tests that were skipped

coveragePercent.description: Overall line coverage percentage (if coverage enabled)

depends.reason: Vitest natively transforms TypeScript via Vite — TypeScript knowledge helps with config and type testing
Understanding mock patterns helps use vi.fn, vi.mock, vi.spyOn effectively

author: dojo-community
